claim: WHO reports 287000 maternal deaths globally in 2020, Sub-Saharan Africa accounting for approximately 70 percent, with lifetime risk exceeding 1 in 40 in highest-burden countries versus 1 in 12000 in high-income countries.
method: Reviewed WHO fact sheet burden estimates, causes, and intervention coverage.
limitations: Maternal mortality estimates are modeled where civil registration is incomplete.
Sub-national estimates may smooth high-risk areas.

claim: The 2018 Lancet Commission on High-Quality Health Systems found facility delivery rates increased but many facilities classified as providing emergency obstetric care lack key signal functions: blood transfusion, cesarean section, magnesium sulfate.

source.title: High-quality health systems in the Sustainable Development Goals era: time for a revolution

method: Reviewed Commission findings on EmOC signal functions, quality measurement, and access gaps.
limitations: Commission reports aggregate data from multiple country studies with varying methods.
Facility readiness data currency varies substantially by country.
